spec.reasoning: This task has risk flags for row insertion/deletion and blank row positions, so blank_row_insert and title_row_insert are unsafe. I chose lexical renames and a distractor column as the two safe perturbation types. Column renames: 'Value' → 'Amount', 'Binaries' → 'Flags', 'Target value :' → 'Goal amount :', 'Solver result' → 'Computed total', 'Difference:' → 'Deviation:'. These maintain the same descriptiveness level. The instruction references 'Value' column, 'Binaries' column, and 'Result values' column by name — 'Value' and 'Binaries' are renamed so the instruction must be updated to use the new names. 'Result values' appears only in the instruction (not as a header) so it likely refers to a data range label; I leave it or note it needs instruction update. The sheet is renamed from 'Sheet1' to 'Data1'. A distractor column 'Notes' is added after the last column to test whether the agent hardcodes column count. No data_value_renames needed since the header names don't appear as data values in the cells (the 'Value' header is a column name, not repeated as a data value in the data rows — the risk flag 'data_values_match_headers' refers to the overlap at A2 which is the header cell itself).
